creator: andy@physast.uga.edu
text:
Running any fortran program compiled with
NAG f95 and MPI immediately segmentation faults.
The operating system is FreeBSD-4.1 RELEASE and
MPI was installed from the ports skeleton available
from the web which is mpich-1.2.1_1.
a gdb backtrace on an included sample program produces :

(gdb) kang.physast.uga.edu $ gdb pi3 pi3.core 
GNU gdb 4.18
Copyright 1998 Free Software Foundation, Inc.
GDB is free software, covered by the GNU General Public License, and you are
welcome to change it and/or distribute copies of it under certain conditions.
Type "show copying" to see the conditions.
There is absolutely no warranty for GDB.  Type "show warranty" for details.
This GDB was configured as "i386-unknown-freebsd"...
Core was generated by `pi3'.
Program terminated with signal 11, Segmentation fault.
Reading symbols from /usr/lib/libg2c.so.1...done.
Reading symbols from /usr/lib/libm.so.2...done.
Reading symbols from /usr/lib/libc.so.4...done.
Reading symbols from /usr/libexec/ld-elf.so.1...done.
#0  0x2810f3d8 in rindex () from /usr/lib/libc.so.4
(gdb) bt
#0  0x2810f3d8 in rindex () from /usr/lib/libc.so.4
#1  0x400000 in ?? ()
#2  0x8053af9 in p4_initenv ()
#3  0x805ca36 in MPID_P4_Init ()
#4  0x805c1e5 in MPID_CH_InitMsgPass ()
#5  0x805a3e9 in MPID_Init ()
#6  0x804a738 in MPIR_Init ()
#7  0x804a638 in PMPI_Init ()
#8  0x804a2e1 in pmpi_init_ ()
#9  0x8049e8a in main ()
#10 0x8049dfd in _start ()

Fix: 

Unknown
How-To-Repeat: 

OS : FreeBSD 4.1 RELEASE,  :
kang.physast.uga.edu $ uname -a
FreeBSD kang.physast.uga.edu 4.1-RELEASE FreeBSD 4.1-RELEASE #0: Fri Jul 28 14:30:31 GMT 2000     jkh@ref4.freebsd.org:/usr/src/sys/compile/GENERIC  i386

Standard gcc compiler for that OS :
kang.physast.uga.edu $ cc -v
Using builtin specs.
gcc version 2.95.2 19991024 (release)

NAG F95 compiler :
kang.physast.uga.edu $ f95 -version
NAGWare Fortran 95 compiler Release 4.0a(304)
Copyright 1990-1999 The Numerical Algorithms Group Ltd., Oxford, U.K.

MPICH version : from the ports collection as of Oct. 12 2000 (mpich-1.2.1_1)
(also happens for the default version). In /usr/ports/net/mpich
$ make patch
Then apply the following trivial but necessary patch to the Makefile :

*** Makefile.orig       Thu Oct 12 15:04:35 2000
--- Makefile.nagf95     Thu Oct 12 17:23:16 2000
***************
*** 19,25 ****
  DIST_SUBDIR=  mpich
  
  HAS_CONFIGURE=        yes
! CONFIGURE_ARGS=       -cflags="${CFLAGS}" -prefix=${PREFIX}/mpich
  
  .include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
  
--- 19,25 ----
  DIST_SUBDIR=  mpich
  
  HAS_CONFIGURE=        yes
! CONFIGURE_ARGS=       -cflags="${CFLAGS}" -prefix=${PREFIX}/mpich --with-nagf95 -fc=f95 -f90=f95 --with-device=ch_p4 --with-arch=freebsd
  
  .include <bsd.port.pre.mk>
  

Which is necesary to get consistent f77 and f95 libraries.
After a make install, any fortran program in the included
/usr/ports/net/mpich/work/mpich-1.2.1/examples/
/usr/ports/net/mpich/work/mpich-1.2.1/examples/test/
directories will do a segmentation fault.

The C programs work, and the Fortran programs
work if g77 is used instead.

Since I don't know if this is a problem with the Fortran
compiler, MPI or the C-library, I send this report
to the FreeBSD developers, to the MPI developers and NAG support.
